1.11 regression: Parallel precompilation recompiles repeatedly","user":{"login":"lukas-weber","id":49278367,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQ5Mjc4MzY3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/49278367?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber","html_url":"https://github.com/lukas-weber","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-11-11T19:32:22Z","updated_at":"2024-11-11T19:32:22Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","sub_issues_summary":{"total":0,"completed":0,"percent_completed":0},"active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I noticed that precompilation between parallel Julia processes, which worked really well since Julia 1.10, has slowed down massively with the release of Julia 1.11. The console output suggests that this is caused by repeated recompilations of packages that have already been precompiled by another process.

# Steps to reproduce
To reproduce this, we can load a package that takes long to precompile, such as MathOptInterface.

Create a file `test.jl` with the contents
```julia 
using MathOptInterface
```
and to make precompilation retrigger easily, we add `MathOptInterface` in development mode and subsequently edit one of its files.
```
] activate .
] dev MathOptInterface
```
To reduce noise, we also first run a global precompilation to make sure all dependencies are precompiled.

Given this preparation we can do the following:

```
julia +1.10 --project -e 'using Pkg; Pkg.precompile()'
echo "# modify" >> ~/.julia/dev/MathOptInterface/src/MathOptInterface.jl
time mpirun -n 5 julia +1.10 --project test.jl
```
which takes 62 secs on my machine. Now repeat for Julia 1.11.1

```
julia +1.11 --project -e 'using Pkg; Pkg.precompile()'
echo "# modify" >> ~/.julia/dev/MathOptInterface/src/MathOptInterface.jl
time mpirun -n 5 julia +1.11 --project test.jl
```
This takes 96 secs and now also produces the log
```
Precompiling project...
  1 dependency successfully precompiled in 52 seconds. 61 already precompiled.
Precompiling MathOptInterface...
Precompiling MathOptInterface...
Precompiling MathOptInterface...
Precompiling MathOptInterface...
Precompiling MathOptInterface...
    MathOptInterface Being precompiled by another process (pid: 1705586, pidfile: /mnt/home/lweber/.julia/compiled/v1.11/MathOptInterface/tyub8_roV3j.ji.pidfile)
    MathOptInterface Being precompiled by another process (pid: 1705586, pidfile: /mnt/home/lweber/.julia/compiled/v1.11/MathOptInterface/tyub8_roV3j.ji.pidfile)
    MathOptInterface Being precompiled by another process (pid: 1705586, pidfile: /mnt/home/lweber/.julia/compiled/v1.11/MathOptInterface/tyub8_roV3j.ji.pidfile)
    MathOptInterface Being precompiled by another process (pid: 1705586, pidfile: /mnt/home/lweber/.julia/compiled/v1.11/MathOptInterface/tyub8_roV3j.ji.pidfile)
  52337.8 ms  ✓ MathOptInterface
  1 dependency successfully precompiled in 55 seconds. 61 already precompiled.
  60610.2 ms  ✓ MathOptInterface
  1 dependency successfully precompiled in 64 seconds. 61 already precompiled.
  70583.5 ms  ✓ MathOptInterface
  1 dependency successfully precompiled in 73 seconds. 61 already precompiled.
  80622.7 ms  ✓ MathOptInterface
  1 dependency successfully precompiled in 84 seconds. 61 already precompiled.
  90628.9 ms  ✓ MathOptInterface
  1 dependency successfully precompiled in 94 seconds. 61 already precompiled.
```
Seemingly there is a 10 second extra cost per MPI rank, which makes precompilation increasingly slower for large parallel runs with hundreds of processes.

The issue persists in the nightly version.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56529/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56529/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

zero-dimensional arrays can turn up as corner cases. It is useful when julia handles them gracefully, e.g. as happens if you take the `sqrt` of a zero-dimensional matrix.

```
julia> sqrt(ones(0,0))
0×0 Matrix{Float64}
```

If the matrix is complex, however, it tries to call LAPACK producing the following error.
```
julia> sqrt(ones(ComplexF64,0,0))
 ** On entry to ZHEEVR parameter number 15 had an illegal value
ERROR: ArgumentError: invalid argument JuliaLang/julia#15 to LAPACK call
Stacktrace:
 [1] chklapackerror(ret::Int64)
   @ LinearAlgebra.LAPACK ~/dl/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.10/LinearAlgebra/src/lapack.jl:38 [inlined]
 [2] syevr!(jobz::Char, range::Char, uplo::Char, A::Matrix{ComplexF64}, vl::Float64, vu::Float64, il::Int64, iu::Int64, abstol::Float64)
   @ LinearAlgebra.LAPACK ~/dl/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.10/LinearAlgebra/src/lapack.jl:5454
 [3] eigen!(A::LinearAlgebra.Hermitian{ComplexF64, Matrix{ComplexF64}}; sortby::Nothing)
   @ LinearAlgebra ~/dl/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.10/LinearAlgebra/src/symmetriceigen.jl:8 [inlined]
 [4] eigen(A::LinearAlgebra.Hermitian{ComplexF64, Matrix{ComplexF64}}; sortby::Nothing)
   @ LinearAlgebra ~/dl/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.10/LinearAlgebra/src/symmetriceigen.jl:13
 [5] eigen(A::LinearAlgebra.Hermitian{ComplexF64, Matrix{ComplexF64}})
   @ LinearAlgebra ~/dl/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.10/LinearAlgebra/src/symmetriceigen.jl:11 [inlined]
 [6] sqrt(A::LinearAlgebra.Hermitian{ComplexF64, Matrix{ComplexF64}}; rtol::Float64)
   @ LinearAlgebra ~/dl/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.10/LinearAlgebra/src/symmetric.jl:870
 [7] sqrt(A::LinearAlgebra.Hermitian{ComplexF64, Matrix{ComplexF64}})
   @ LinearAlgebra ~/dl/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.10/LinearAlgebra/src/symmetric.jl:868 [inlined]
 [8] sqrt(A::Matrix{ComplexF64})
   @ LinearAlgebra ~/dl/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.10/LinearAlgebra/src/dense.jl:879
 [9] top-level scope
   @ REPL[9]:1
```

I tested and found this behavior in Julia 1.8.3, 1.8.5 and the current master branch.
```
julia> versioninfo()
Julia Version 1.10.0-DEV.1233
Commit 83225c510a (2023-05-06 11:35 UTC)
DEBUG build
Platform Info:
  OS: Linux (x86_64-pc-linux-gnu)

  WORD_SIZE: 64
  LIBM: libopenlibm
  LLVM: libLLVM-14.0.6 (ORCJIT, alderlake)
  Threads: 1 on 20 virtual cores

parts of julia linear algebra handle empty matrices gracefully, which is helpful when writing codes where they appear as a corner case. However, dividing LU-decompositions of generic numbers will yield a SingularException instead.

```julia
using LinearAlgebra
import ForwardDiff: Dual

a = fill(Dual(1.0, 1.0), 0, 0)
v = fill(Dual(1.0, 1.0), 0, 10) 

a \ v # return 0x0 matrix as expected

lu(a) \ v # throws SingularException(0)
```
```
ERROR: SingularException(0)
Stacktrace:

```

It is worth noting that doing the same with *complex* Dual numbers on 1.10.1 will lead to segfaults as well. In the nightly, I was not able to reproduce that so I assume it has been fixed.

```julia
using LinearAlgebra
import ForwardDiff: Dual

a = fill(complex(Dual(1.0, 1.0)), 0, 0)
v = fill(complex(Dual(1.0, 1.0)), 0, 10) 

a \ v # return 0x0 matrix as expected

lu(a) \ v # throws SingularException(0)
```
```
ERROR: SingularException(0)
Stacktrace:

```

It is worth noting that doing the same with *complex* Dual numbers on 1.10.1 will lead to segfaults as well. In the nightly, I was not able to reproduce that so I assume it has been fixed.

```julia
using LinearAlgebra
import ForwardDiff: Dual

a = fill(complex(Dual(1.0, 1.0)), 0, 0)
v = fill(complex(Dual(1.0, 1.0)), 0, 10) 

a \ v # return 0x0 matrix as expected

lu(a) \ v # throws SingularException(0)
```

noticed that the following is type unstable (on v3.3.3 and julia 1.10.0)

```julia
using RecursiveArrayTools

sum(ArrayPartition(ArrayPartition(zeros(4,4)))) # ::Any

sum(VectorOfArray([zeros(4), VectorOfArray([zeros(4,4)])])) # ::Any
```

Curiously, all of the following are stable.
```julia
sum(ArrayPartition(ArrayPartition(zeros(4)))) # ::Float64
sum(ArrayPartition(zeros(4,4))) # ::Float64

sum(VectorOfArray([VectorOfArray([zeros(4,4)])])) # ::Float64
```

The other recursive array types seem to be affected as well. Not sure if getting these stable is in-scope, but it would be super helpful.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/RecursiveArrayTools.jl/issues/323/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/SciML/RecursiveArrayTools.jl/issues/323/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Distributed.jl/issues/13","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Distributed.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Distributed.jl/issues/13/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Distributed.jl/issues/13/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Distributed.jl/issues/13/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/Distributed.jl/issues/13","id":2106419634,"node_id":"I_kwDOKdArvc59jWmy","number":13,"title":"Spurious `@spawnat` parallelism with single worker, single thread","user":{"login":"lukas-weber","id":49278367,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQ5Mjc4MzY3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/49278367?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber","html_url":"https://github.com/lukas-weber","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2024-01-29T21:02:00Z","updated_at":"2024-01-29T21:02:00Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Consider the following

```julia
using Distributed

function f()
    println("start")
    sleep(2)
    println("end")
end

fs = [@spawnat :any f() for _ = 1:4]

fetch.(fs)
```

When starting julia without any arguments, I see that `nworkers() == 1` and `Threads.num_threads() == 1`, so I would expect the above example to run serially, since I have not provided any resources for parallel execution. What happens though is

```
start
start
start
start
[2 second wait]
end
end
end
end
```

This might be intended behavior, but to me it is quite confusing and inconvenient since I seemingly have no way to regulate the resource consumption with this setup. Has it always been like this?","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Distributed.jl/issues/13/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Distributed.jl/issues/13/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Distributed.jl/issues/13","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Distributed.jl","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Distributed.jl/issues/13/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Distributed.jl/issues/13/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/Distributed.jl/issues/13/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/Distributed.jl/issues/13","id":2106419634,"node_id":"I_kwDOKdArvc59jWmy","number":13,"title":"Spurious `@spawnat` parallelism with single worker, single thread","user":{"login":"lukas-weber","id":49278367,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQ5Mjc4MzY3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/49278367?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber","html_url":"https://github.com/lukas-weber","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"closed","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-01-29T21:02:00Z","updated_at":"2024-01-29T21:47:45Z","closed_at":"2024-01-29T21:47:44Z","author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Consider the following

```julia
using Distributed

@everywhere function f()
    println("start")
    sleep(2)
    println("end")
end

fs = [@spawnat :any f() for _ = 1:4]

fetch.(fs)
```

When starting julia without any arguments, I see that `nworkers() == 1` and `Threads.num_threads() == 1`, so I would expect the above example to run serially, since I have not provided any resources for parallel execution. What happens though is

```
start
start
start
start
[2 second wait]
end
end
end
end
```

This might be intended behavior, but to me it is quite confusing and inconvenient since I seemingly have no way to regulate the resource consumption with this setup. {"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49667","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49667/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49667/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49667/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49667","id":1698621405,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM5lPufd","number":49667,"title":"LinearAlgebra: LAPACK error on sqrt of zero-dimensional complex matrices","user":{"login":"lukas-weber","id":49278367,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQ5Mjc4MzY3","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/49278367?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber","html_url":"https://github.com/lukas-weber","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/lukas-weber/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2023-05-06T12:58:26Z","updated_at":"2023-05-06T12:58:26Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"Sometimes, zero-dimensional arrays can turn up as corner cases. It is useful when julia handles them gracefully, e.g. as happens if you take the `sqrt` of a zero-dimensional matrix.

```
julia> sqrt(ones(0,0))
0×0 Matrix{Float64}
```

If the matrix is complex, however, it tries to call LAPACK producing the following error.
```
julia> sqrt(ones(ComplexF64,0,0))
 ** On entry to ZHEEVR parameter number 15 had an illegal value
ERROR: ArgumentError: invalid argument #15 to LAPACK call
Stacktrace:
 [1] chklapackerror(ret::Int64)
   @ LinearAlgebra.LAPACK ~/dl/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.10/LinearAlgebra/src/lapack.jl:38 [inlined]
 [2] syevr!(jobz::Char, range::Char, uplo::Char, A::Matrix{ComplexF64}, vl::Float64, vu::Float64, il::Int64, iu::Int64, abstol::Float64)
   @ LinearAlgebra.LAPACK ~/dl/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.10/LinearAlgebra/src/lapack.jl:5454
 [3] eigen!(A::LinearAlgebra.Hermitian{ComplexF64, Matrix{ComplexF64}}; sortby::Nothing)
   @ LinearAlgebra ~/dl/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.10/LinearAlgebra/src/symmetriceigen.jl:8 [inlined]
 [4] eigen(A::LinearAlgebra.Hermitian{ComplexF64, Matrix{ComplexF64}}; sortby::Nothing)
   @ LinearAlgebra ~/dl/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.10/LinearAlgebra/src/symmetriceigen.jl:13
 [5] eigen(A::LinearAlgebra.Hermitian{ComplexF64, Matrix{ComplexF64}})
   @ LinearAlgebra ~/dl/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.10/LinearAlgebra/src/symmetriceigen.jl:11 [inlined]
 [6] sqrt(A::LinearAlgebra.Hermitian{ComplexF64, Matrix{ComplexF64}}; rtol::Float64)
   @ LinearAlgebra ~/dl/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.10/LinearAlgebra/src/symmetric.jl:870
 [7] sqrt(A::LinearAlgebra.Hermitian{ComplexF64, Matrix{ComplexF64}})
   @ LinearAlgebra ~/dl/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.10/LinearAlgebra/src/symmetric.jl:868 [inlined]
 [8] sqrt(A::Matrix{ComplexF64})
   @ LinearAlgebra ~/dl/julia/usr/share/julia/stdlib/v1.10/LinearAlgebra/src/dense.jl:879
 [9] top-level scope
   @ REPL[9]:1
```

I tested and found this behavior in Julia 1.8.3, 1.8.5 and the current master branch.
```
julia> versioninfo()
Julia Version 1.10.0-DEV.1233
Commit 83225c510a (2023-05-06 11:35 UTC)
DEBUG build
Platform Info:
  OS: Linux (x86_64-pc-linux-gnu)
  CPU: 20 × 12th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-1280P
  WORD_SIZE: 64
  LIBM: libopenlibm
  LLVM: libLLVM-14.0.6 (ORCJIT, alderlake)
  Threads: 1 on 20 virtual cores
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49667/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49667/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null}}
